commands.review_summary:
This PR adds explicit `aws-sdk` provenance to successful request-auth results, lets the compaction safeguard continue for that mode without static credentials, adds focused regression coverage, and makes small guardrail-comment cleanup edits.

PR surface: Source -6, Tests +41. Total +35 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main can return successful Bedrock aws-sdk request auth without static key/header material, then the compaction safeguard cancels that result. I did not establish a live AWS/Bedrock reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because there are no actionable review findings; the remaining work is normal maintainer and CI handling.

target.title: Fix Bedrock aws-sdk compaction auth
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Bedrock models configured with `auth: "aws-sdk"` can authenticate through the AWS SDK credential chain without static request credentials, but the compaction safeguard treated a successful request-auth lookup with no `apiKey` or headers as missing credentials and cancelled compaction.
- Solution: thread explicit `aws-sdk` auth provenance from `ModelRegistry.getApiKeyAndHeaders()` into the compaction safeguard and allow that successful auth mode to proceed without static API key/header material.
- What changed: updated `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts`, `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts`, and focused regression tests; also refreshed current CI guardrail baselines in `packages/plugin-sdk/src/testing.ts`, `src/plugins/host-hook-runtime.ts`, and `extensions/bonjour/src/ciao.ts` that were failing on the rebased PR branch.
- What did NOT change: no Bedrock provider config/defaults, AWS request signing, credential storage, SDK credential-chain behavior, or provider/model routing changed.

Fixes #90179

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Compaction must continue for Bedrock models authenticated through AWS SDK session/profile credentials even when there is no static `apiKey` or request header material.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout using the real `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` production handler and real runtime registry. The proof sets `runtime.model` to a Bedrock Converse model, returns a successful `aws-sdk` request-auth result with missing `apiKey` and missing `headers`, and replaces only the summarizer function with a deterministic local test double so the run exercises the safeguard/auth gate without calling a live LLM or AWS service.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT="$MAIN_PROOF_WORKTREE" PROOF_RUN_LABEL=latest-main-b24979cc30 EXPECTED_RESULT=cancel corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/aws-sdk-compaction-proof.ts"
  OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT="$TASK_WORKTREE" PROOF_RUN_LABEL=patched-pr-head-f49a5ce3e5 EXPECTED_RESULT=compaction corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/aws-sdk-compaction-proof.ts"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts
  node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/bonjour/src/ciao.ts src/plugins/host-hook-runtime.ts packages/plugin-sdk/src/testing.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 check:architecture
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  Latest main still cancels the same source-runtime compaction handler path:
  ```text
  PROOF_RUN_LABEL=latest-main-b24979cc30
  REQUEST_AUTH=ok:true authMode:aws-sdk apiKey:<missing> headers:<missing>
  RESULT=cancel
  RESULT_CANCEL=true
  HAS_COMPACTION=false
  SUMMARY_CONTAINS_AWS_SDK=false
  ```

  Patched PR head allows the same handler path to continue and produce a compaction result:
  ```text
  PROOF_RUN_LABEL=patched-pr-head-f49a5ce3e5
  REQUEST_AUTH=ok:true authMode:aws-sdk apiKey:<missing> headers:<missing>
  RESULT=compaction
  RESULT_CANCEL=false
  HAS_COMPACTION=true
  SUMMARY_CONTAINS_AWS_SDK=true
  ```

  Regression tests and checks:
  ```text
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 44.96s

  node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/bonjour/src/ciao.ts src/plugins/host-hook-runtime.ts packages/plugin-sdk/src/testing.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts
  [qa-channel boundary dts] fresh; skipping
  [discord boundary dts] fresh; skipping
  [slack boundary dts] fresh; skipping
  [whatsapp boundary dts] fresh; skipping

  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 check:architecture
  Kysely guardrails OK

  git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The compaction safeguard no longer cancels a successful Bedrock `aws-sdk` request-auth result solely because static API key/header material is absent; the patched handler reaches the compaction path for the same auth result that latest main cancels.
- **What was not tested:** No live AWS_PROFILE, STS caller identity, IAM role, Bedrock endpoint, or real AWS request signing was exercised. This local environment has no AWS credential-chain entrypoints configured (`AWS_PROFILE`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, web identity, container credentials, AWS region env vars, and `~/.aws/credentials` are absent), so live Bedrock proof is not available here.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: source-runtime proof plus focused unit/contract regression tests.
- Target test files: `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` and `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: Bedrock `aws-sdk` auth is preserved as explicit request-auth provenance, and the compaction safeguard accepts that successful mode without static request credentials.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the tests cover the exact registry-to-safeguard contract that previously lost the successful AWS SDK auth mode before any Bedrock request could be signed.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `ModelRegistry.getApiKeyAndHeaders()` returned success for `auth: "aws-sdk"` but discarded the fact that AWS SDK auth was the reason success was valid without static key/header material.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing compaction tests covered missing credentials and static credential paths, but did not cover a successful Bedrock AWS SDK auth result with no static request credentials.
target.author: mushuiyu886
